原文:
Personal Forgiveness

Taking responsibility for mistakes is a positive① step, but don't beat yourself up about them. To err(犯错) is human. D. It's just as important to show yourself some forgiveness②. You can use the following writing exercise to help you do this.
翻译:
个人宽恕

为错误承担责任是积极的一步，但不要为此过分自责。犯错是人之常情。D. 同样重要的是，要对自己表现出一些宽恕。你可以使用以下的写作练习来帮助你做到这一点。
必背词汇:
① positive /ˈpɒzətɪv/ adj. 积极的；肯定的
Ⓡ position /pəˈzɪʃn/ n. 位置；职位
Ⓡ oppose /əˈpəʊz/ v. 反对
Ⓡ opposite /ˈɒpəzɪt/ adj. 相反的
Ⓡ compose /kəmˈpəʊz/ v. 组成；创作
② forgiveness /fəˈɡɪvnəs/ n. 宽恕；原谅
Ⓡ forgive /fəˈɡɪv/ v. 原谅；宽恕
Ⓡ given /ˈɡɪvn/ adj. 特定的；给予的
Ⓡ forget /fəˈɡet/ v. 忘记
Ⓡ forgetful /fəˈɡetfl/ adj. 健忘的

原文: In a journal or on a piece of paper, put the heading "Personal strengths." B. Now list all the characteristics③ you like about yourself. Are you caring? Creative? Generous? A good listener? Fun to be around? They don't have to be world-changing, just aspects④ of your personality that you're proud of.
翻译: 在日记本或一张纸上，写下标题“个人优势”。B. 现在列出所有你喜欢的自身特质。你是有爱心吗？有创造力吗？慷慨大方吗？是一个好的倾听者吗？有趣好相处吗？它们不必是改变世界的，只是你个性中让你自豪的方面。
必背词汇:
③ characteristic /ˌkærəktəˈrɪstɪk/ n. 特征；特性 adj. 典型的
Ⓡ character /ˈkærəktə(r)/ n. 性格；角色
Ⓡ characterize /ˈkærəktəraɪz/ v. 以...为特征
Ⓡ characteristic of 是...的特征
④ aspect /ˈæspekt/ n. 方面；外观
Ⓡ respect /rɪˈspekt/ n./v. 尊重
Ⓡ inspect /ɪnˈspekt/ v. 检查
Ⓡ prospect /ˈprɒspekt/ n. 前景
Ⓡ perspective /pəˈspektɪv/ n. 视角

原文: At the top of a second page, put the heading "Acts of kindness." On this one, list all the positive things you've done for others. It might be the time when you helped a friend with their homework, when you did the ironing without being asked, or when you baked cookies after the family had had a tiring⑤ day. F. Whatever it is, no matter how small it might seem, write it down.
翻译: 在第二页的顶部，写下标题“善行”。在这一页上，列出所有你为他人做过的积极事情。可能是你帮助朋友做作业的时候，未经要求就熨烫衣服的时候，或者在家人在度过疲惫的一天后烤饼干的时候。F. 无论是什么，无论它看起来多么微小，都把它写下来。
必背词汇:
⑤ tiring /ˈtaɪərɪŋ/ adj. 令人疲劳的；累人的
Ⓡ tire /ˈtaɪə(r)/ v. (使)疲劳 n. 轮胎
Ⓡ tired /ˈtaɪəd/ adj. 疲劳的；厌倦的
Ⓡ tiresome /ˈtaɪəsəm/ adj. 令人厌烦的
Ⓡ retire /rɪˈtaɪə(r)/ v. 退休

原文: You could ask a friend or family member to help add to your list. C. They might even like to have a go at doing the exercise. That way, you could exchange thoughts on what makes each of you special and the aspects of your personality that shine through. In fact, don't wait until you've made a mistake to try this—it's a great way to boost⑥ self-confidence at any time.
翻译: 你可以请朋友或家人帮忙补充你的列表。C. 他们甚至可能喜欢尝试做这个练习。这样，你们可以交流是什么让彼此特别，以及你们个性中闪耀的方面。事实上，不要等到犯了错才尝试这个——这是在任何时候提升自信的好方法。
必背词汇:
⑥ boost /buːst/ v. 提升；促进 n. 推动
Ⓡ boast /bəʊst/ v. 自夸
Ⓡ boost up 提升；增强
Ⓡ boost confidence 增强信心

原文: It's something of a cliché（陈词滥调） that most people learn not from their successes but their mistakes. The thing is, it's true. G. Whatever the mistake, remember it isn't a fixed⑦ aspect of your personality. We're all changing and learning all the time and mistakes are a positive way to develop⑧ and grow.
翻译: 大多数人不是从成功中学习，而是从错误中学习，这有点陈词滥调。但事实是，这是真的。G. 无论是什么错误，记住它不是你的个性中固定不变的方面。我们都在不断变化和学习，错误是发展和成长的积极方式。
必背词汇:
⑦ fixed /fɪkst/ adj. 固定的；不变的
Ⓡ fix /fɪks/ v. 固定；修理
Ⓡ fixture /ˈfɪkstʃə(r)/ n. 固定装置
Ⓡ prefix /ˈpriːfɪks/ n. 前缀
Ⓡ suffix /ˈsʌfɪks/ n. 后缀
⑧ develop /dɪˈveləp/ v. 发展；开发；成长
Ⓡ development /dɪˈveləpmənt/ n. 发展；开发
Ⓡ developing /dɪˈveləpɪŋ/ adj. 发展中的
Ⓡ developed /dɪˈveləpt/ adj. 发达的
Ⓡ developer /dɪˈveləpə(r)/ n. 开发者
